title: Proposal and Quote Follow-Up for Law Firms
metaDescription: Done-for-you proposal and quote follow-up for law firms. Engagement letters and fee quotes get chased on schedule, in your voice, until the client signs.
excerpt: Most law firms lose work because engagement letters sit unread. This AIOS Capability chases every proposal and quote on a schedule, in your voice, until the client decides.

sections.intro:
If you run a law firm, you have seen this pattern. You meet a prospective client, scope the matter, and send an engagement letter or a fixed-fee quote. Then nothing. The client gets pulled into their own work, your proposal slides down their inbox, and a week later you are not sure if you should nudge them again or leave it alone. Partners and associates are billing on live matters, so chasing quotes drops to the bottom of the list.

Proposal and Quote Follow-Up is a Capability inside your AI Operating System. Once an engagement letter or fee quote goes out, the AIOS tracks it, follows up on a clear cadence in your voice, answers basic questions, and routes anything sensitive back to the responsible attorney. You stop losing matters to silence, and you stop feeling pushy.
sections.fourCFit:
This page is about the Capabilities layer of your AIOS. Capabilities are the done-for-you workflows that draft, route, summarize, follow up, and report. Proposal and Quote Follow-Up only works because the other three layers are in place:

  • Context: the system knows your practice areas, fee structures, standard engagement terms, and the tone partners use with clients.
  • Connections: it reads from your inbox, document system, CRM or intake tool, and calendar, so it knows which quote went to whom and whether they have replied or booked a call.
  • Capabilities: this follow-up workflow itself, sitting alongside intake triage, conflict-check reminders, and matter status updates.
  • Cadence: the system runs follow-ups on schedule, including evenings and weekends, without an attorney pressing send.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
When this Capability is installed, here is the concrete artifact running in your firm:

  • Every engagement letter or fee quote you send is logged as an open opportunity with the responsible attorney, matter type, fee amount, and date sent.
  • A follow-up sequence runs automatically: a short check-in around day 2 or 3, a value reminder around day 7, and a clear close-out message around day 14.
  • Each message is drafted in the sending attorney's voice and references the specific matter, not a generic template.
  • Replies are read and classified. Signed or accepted quotes are moved to onboarding. Questions about scope, fees, or timing are summarized and routed to the responsible attorney.
  • Anything that looks like a complaint, a conflict issue, or a legal question gets paused and flagged for human review before any reply goes out.
  • You get a weekly report: open proposals, accepted, declined, silent, and total dollars in play.
  • SLA: no proposal sits longer than 72 hours without a touch, and no client reply waits longer than one business hour during firm hours.
sections.howWeInstall:
Installation follows the standard AIOS three-phase path. You stay in control the whole time, and the system is yours.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes). We look at your current intake, how proposals and engagement letters get sent, and where matters are stalling. You leave with a short written plan even if you never hire us. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days). We wire the Capability into your existing inbox, document system, and CRM. We capture your firm's tone from real past emails, write the follow-up sequences, set the routing rules for sensitive replies, and test the whole flow on live but supervised proposals.
  3. Operate (ongoing). The system runs every day. We add one new automation per week, tune the messages based on what is converting, and adjust cadence as your practice mix shifts. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
Based on what we see with professional services firms of similar size, here is a realistic range once this Capability has been running for 60 to 90 days:

  • Proposal acceptance rates lift by 10 to 25 percent, mostly by recovering matters that would have gone silent.
  • Time from quote sent to signed engagement drops from an average of 10 to 14 days down to 3 to 6 days.
  • Attorneys recover 4 to 8 hours per week that used to go into manual follow-up, status checking, and writing chase emails.
  • The firm has a clear weekly view of pipeline value, which is often the first time partners have seen one.

Standing guarantee: across your full AIOS, if we do not help you recover 40 or more hours a month within 90 days, we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Will follow-up messages sound like a bot or pressure the client?

No. We capture the sending attorney's actual tone from past emails before we write a single sequence. The messages are short, professional, and assume the client is busy, not avoiding you. You approve every template before it goes live.

What happens if a client replies with a legal question or a complaint?

The system never answers legal questions on your behalf. Anything that looks like substantive legal content, a fee dispute, or a complaint is paused and routed to the responsible attorney with a short summary. Only routine replies, like scheduling or simple scope clarifications, get a drafted response, and even those can be set to require attorney approval.

Do we have to change our case management or document software?

No. The AIOS is tool-agnostic and works with the systems you already use for intake, documents, billing, and email. If your stack changes later, the Capability moves with you.
